entity.name: DOJ Antitrust Division
entity.description: Investigates AI-market concentration with FTC under a jurisdictional split (DOJ took Nvidia, Microsoft; FTC took OpenAI, Anthropic, Google). Live merger reviews shape compute-market structure; Google search remedies trial sets precedent for AI-product distribution mandates.

activity.title: Notice Pursuant to the National Cooperative Research and Production Act of 1993-Biopharmaceutical Manufacturing Preparedness Consortium
activity.raw_text: Notice Pursuant to the National Cooperative Research and Production Act of 1993-Biopharmaceutical Manufacturing Preparedness Consortium

enrichment.summary: The DOJ Antitrust Division issued a notice pursuant to the National Cooperative Research and Production Act of 1993 regarding the Biopharmaceutical Manufacturing Preparedness Consortium. This is a procedural filing that logs the consortium's membership or activities with the DOJ to receive limited antitrust protection under the NCRPA. The notice itself does not take a substantive policy position on AI or related topics.

activity.title: United States et al. v. RealPage, Inc.et al.; Response of the United States to Public Comments
activity.raw_text: United States et al. v. RealPage, Inc.et al.; Response of the United States to Public Comments

enrichment.summary: The DOJ Antitrust Division filed a response to public comments in the United States et al. v. RealPage, Inc. et al. case, published in the Federal Register. RealPage is a software company whose algorithmic pricing tools for rental housing have been the subject of antitrust scrutiny. This filing represents a procedural step in the consent decree or settlement process under the Tunney Act, where the government responds to public input before a court enters a final judgment.

activity.title: United States of America et al. v. RealPage, Inc. et al. Proposed Final Judgment and Competitive Impact Statement
activity.raw_text: United States of America et al. v. RealPage, Inc. et al. Proposed Final Judgment and Competitive Impact Statement

enrichment.summary: The DOJ Antitrust Division published a Proposed Final Judgment and Competitive Impact Statement in United States of America et al. v. RealPage, Inc. et al. This filing reflects the government's proposed resolution of its antitrust case against RealPage, a company that provides algorithmic rent-setting software used by landlords. The document outlines proposed remedies and assesses their competitive impact, as required under the Antitrust Procedures and Penalties Act before a consent decree can be finalized.

activity.title: United States et al. v. Constellation Energy Corporation et al.; Proposed Final Judgment and Competitive Impact Statement
activity.raw_text: United States et al. v. Constellation Energy Corporation et al.; Proposed Final Judgment and Competitive Impact Statement

enrichment.summary: The DOJ Antitrust Division filed a Proposed Final Judgment and Competitive Impact Statement in United States et al. v. Constellation Energy Corporation et al., a civil antitrust case. The filing outlines proposed remedies to address competitive concerns raised by the government regarding Constellation Energy's conduct or a transaction. The Proposed Final Judgment, if entered by the court, would impose binding obligations on the defendants to resolve the antitrust violations alleged by the United States.

activity.title: United States of America et al. v. RealPage, Inc. et al.; Proposed Final Judgment and Competitive Impact Statement
activity.raw_text: United States of America et al. v. RealPage, Inc. et al.; Proposed Final Judgment and Competitive Impact Statement

enrichment.summary: The DOJ Antitrust Division filed a Proposed Final Judgment and Competitive Impact Statement in United States of America et al. v. RealPage, Inc. et al. The case concerns anticompetitive conduct by RealPage, Inc., and the proposed judgment sets out the terms of a negotiated resolution. The filing appears in the Federal Register as required under the Antitrust Procedures and Penalties Act before the court enters a final order.

activity.title: United States v. Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. and Juniper Networks, Inc.; Response of the United States to Public Comments on the Proposed Final Judgments
activity.raw_text: United States v. Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. and Juniper Networks, Inc.; Response of the United States to Public Comments on the Proposed Final Judgments

enrichment.summary: The DOJ Antitrust Division filed a response to public comments on the proposed final judgments in United States v. Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. and Juniper Networks, Inc. This is a merger review proceeding in which the government has proposed final judgments to resolve antitrust concerns arising from the acquisition. The response addresses public commentary on the adequacy of the proposed remedies.
